title: MCP Security, Prompt Injection, and Supply Chain Trust · Extension System Mission
summary: An internal service operations group. The group needs a least-privilege remote server resilient to injection, confused deputy, SSRF, and credential leakage. Planned artifact: Deliver Authorized Remote Operations MCP with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement protected resource metadata and OAuth-aligned authorization for remote HTTP.
Validate token audience, issuer, scopes, expiry, and resource binding.
Bind actions to user intent, authorization, audience, and protected resource.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
